opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My calf muscle suddenly locked up into a rock-hard knot, and the pain is so intense I thought the muscle tore in half.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It seized up right after a long run and won't fully let go — I can't put weight on it.

symptom_narrative.onset: 90 minutes ago, right after a 15km run in warm weather
symptom_narrative.character: intense involuntary cramp, hard knot in the calf
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: eases with stretch then recurs at rest
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: sweated a lot on the run; no chest pain, no breathlessness

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; intense endurance exercise with heavy sweating and limited fluids

objective_data_offered.value: visible/palpable hard knot in the right calf; eases a little with stretch
foot warm, can feel and wiggle toes normally
